title: My Newborn Refusing to Eat Challenged Everything I Thought I Knew
description:
“Inborn” by Colleen Morrissey My daughter was born gasping. Throughout the first 14 hours of my labor, Nora was in the ideal position for birth: upside down and facing backwards, towards my spine. But as she entered the birth canal, she corkscrewed. As though she wanted one last look around the only home she’d ever […]

The post My Newborn Refusing to Eat Challenged Everything I Thought I Knew appeared first
hook: When her newborn refused to eat, a mother's certainty about her own body shattered—a visceral reckoning with what we think we know.

title: Paul Klee painted it. Walter Benjamin coveted it. And Morgan Meis spent a lifetime obsessing over it
description: Paul Klee painted it. Walter Benjamin coveted it. And Morgan Meis spent a lifetime obsessing over it
hook: One man spent a lifetime obsessing over a painting that Walter Benjamin coveted. This is what that obsession revealed about desire and history.

title: Dangerous, Dirty, Violent & Young by Zayd Ayers Dohrn review – child of the revolution
description:
The son of fugitive leaders of the militant Weather Underground recounts his chaotic, peripatetic upbringing

Every aspect of a family’s life will seem normal to the small children within it; only hindsight can bring what was abnormal into relief. Zayd Ayers Dohrn’s earliest years were spent on the run from the FBI; his parents were members of the revolutionary Weather Underground faction, a group dedicated to the overthrow of the US government.

By the age of three he had
hook: Born into chaos—a fugitive family, constant movement, revolutionary upheaval—he finally makes sense of the abnormal by writing it down.

title: What The Bear Has Always Been Driving Toward
description: In its final season, the FX show circles its end point.
hook: The Bear knows exactly where it's going. This final season is the destination the entire show has been building toward.

title: Middle of Somewhere
description: "One remote highway in Nevada links far-flung communities that attracted settlers for generations. Why are some still drawn there today?"
hook: A Nevada highway connects communities most people flee. Why do some keep coming back? The answer is stranger than escape.
